by
  -- Since U is countable, we can embed it into ℕ.
  -- Any subset A of U can then be embedded into ℕ as well, making A countable.
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    exact h.subset (by inferred_instance)

h.mono A (Set.subset_univ A)

by
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    have h₂ : SetLike A A := by infer_instance

by
  -- Use the fact that any subset of a countable type is countable.
  have h₁ : ctble A := by
    -- The subset A of U is countable because U is countable.
    exact Set.countable_subset h (by simp [Set.mem_univ])
  exact h₁

by
  have h_main : ctble A := by
    have h₁ : Set.Countable A := by
      -- Since U is countable, any subset of U is countable.
      have h₂ : Set.Countable (Set.univ : Set U) := by
        simpa using h
      -- A is a subset of U, so it is countable.
      exact Set.Countable.mono (by intro x hx; trivial) h₂
    -- Convert the Set.Countable instance to the ctble typeclass.
    simpa [ctble] using h₁
  exact h_main

by
  have h₁ : ctble (A : Type) := by
    apply ctble.of_injective (fun (x : A) => (x : U))
    intro x y hxy
    cases x <;> cases y <;> simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
  exact h₁

by
  -- Use the fact that A is a subset of U and U is countable to deduce that A is countable.
  have h₁ : ctble (A : Set U) := by
    -- Since U is countable, any subset of U is also countable.
    apply Set.countable.mono (Set.subset_univ A)
    -- The universal set of U is countable because U is countable.
    simpa using h
  -- The result follows directly from the above statement.
  exact h₁

      exact Set.countable.mono (fun x hx => hx) h₁

    exact h₃
  exact h₂

by
  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
  refine' ⟨fun x => f x.val, _⟩
  -- We need to show that the function `fun x => f x.val` is injective on `A`.
  -- Since `f` is injective and `x.val` maps distinct elements of `A` to distinct elements of `U`, the composition `f ∘ x.val` will also be injective.
  rintro ⟨x, hx⟩ ⟨y, hy⟩ hxy
  apply Subtype.ext
  -- Simplify the equation `f x.val = f y.val` using the injectivity of `f`.
  apply hf
  simpa using hxy

by
  have h' : ctble (Subtype (fun (x : U) => x ∈ A)) := by
    -- Since U is countable and A is a subset of U, the subtype of U corresponding to A is also countable.
    -- This follows from the fact that subtypes of countable types are countable.
    apply ctble_of_injective (fun (x : Subtype (fun (x : U) => x ∈ A)) => ⟨x.val, ‹_›⟩)
    <;> simp_all [Subtype.ext_iff]
    <;> aesop
  -- The countability of the subtype implies the countability of the set A.
  simpa [h'.out] using h'

  have h₁ : ctble (A : Set U) := by
